changes.value:
## What

Update the `entity-created` handler in `rust-engine-container.tsx` to use the `fields` from the event payload when available (the watcher already populates these for new files). Only fall back to `get_entity` when `fields` is empty (e.g. the entity was created by a store event before the watcher detected it).

This is NOT the same as the enrichment anti-pattern. The watcher reads the new file from disk to build its cache and naturally has the fields. The backend isn't doing an extra read — the fields are a byproduct of cache population.

### Current behavior

`entity-created` handler (line 205-254) always calls `invoke("get_entity", ...)` regardless of whether the event payload has fields.

### Fix

1. Destructure `fields` from `event.payload`
2. If `fields` has entries (`Object.keys(fields).length > 0`): construct entity directly as `{ id, entity_type, fields }` and add to store — no IPC round-trip
3. If `fields` is empty: fall back to `get_entity` (entity created by store before watcher cache populated)
4. Keep the `column` → full refresh special case as-is

### Files to modify

1. **`kanban-app/ui/src/components/rust-engine-container.tsx`** (entity-created listener, ~line 205)
   - Destructure `fields` from event payload
   - Add fast path when fields is populated
   - Keep `get_entity` as fallback for empty fields

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `entity-created` with populated fields adds entity without IPC round-trip
- [ ] `entity-created` with empty fields falls back to `get_entity`
- [ ] "Add Task" button immediately shows new task card
- [ ] Column creation still triggers full refresh
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run` passes

## Tests

- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/rust-engine-container.test.tsx` — test: entity-created with populated fields adds entity without invoke call
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/rust-engine-container.test.tsx` — test: entity-created with empty fields calls get_entity
- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run` — all pass

## Workflow
- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
Frontend: entity-created uses get_entity once for new entities, uses fields payload when available
